what: Use LLM to fill in missing title, category, tags, description, rules, and required_knowledge for each parsed entry
why: Files without standard frontmatter (e.g. non-md formats or custom schemas) need metadata generated before import

success_handling.criteria: All entries have non-null title and category after enrichment (LLM or fallback)

error_handling.action: Apply fallback: filename → title, parent_folder → category; non-fatal

error_handling.scenario: LLM call fails for a specific file
error_handling.criteria: callAnthropicApi throws or returns malformed JSON
error_handling.action: Apply fallback for that file, continue with remaining files

decisions.rationale: Import should succeed even without LLM credentials; fallback metadata (filename + folder) is better than skipping the file entirely
If a file already has title or category in its frontmatter, keep it — LLM is only used to fill gaps
Metadata generation is a simple structured output task; Haiku is fast and cheap enough to run per-file
